{{Quote|Before Alderaan was destroyed by the Death Star, Bail Organa had made a list of locations for the Alliance to operate from in the event that we might lose our base on Yavin IV. He encrypted the information in a data holocron which was encased in phrik, one of the strongest substances in the galaxy. Strong enough even to have survived Alderaan's destruction. We needed that Holocron. The information on it was vital to the survival of the Alliance.|Col Serra to [[Tionne Solusar]]|<ref name="Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron" />}}
A [[Realspace/Legends|space]] '''battle''' between the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] occurred in [[the Graveyard]], the remains of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] after it was [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroyed]] by the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] around [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Shortly after the destruction of that [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] in the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], a Rebel [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] centered around [[Renegade Squadron/Legends|Renegade Squadron]]—a recently established Alliance [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] made up of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] and criminals—attempted to secure a very valuable [[Datacron/Legends|data holocron]] believed to have survived the destruction of Alderaan. The Alliance fleet, under the command of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], believed the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] contained ideal locations for a new base for the Alliance. Likewise, the Empire wanted the holocron to suit their own ends. Upon arrival in the [[Alderaan system/Legends|system]], Solo's fleet found themselves opposed by an Imperial fleet, as well as by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], who had been hired by the Empire to capture the holocron.

After a brief battle between the two forces, the Renegades were able to find and secure the data holocron. After they did so, Fett, aboard his personal [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']], tried to steal the holocron by engaging the Rebels in a [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfight]]. The Rebels were able to defeat the bounty hunter, and destroyed the [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]] on an Imperial [[Interdictor-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Interdictor Star Destroyer]] which was keeping the Alliance fleet trapped in the [[Star system/Legends|system]]. The Alliance retreated from the system, and later used the holocron to find the graveyard [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Boz Pity/Legends|Boz Pity]], where they built a new base.

==Prelude==
Shortly after the [[Evacuation of Yavin]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]],<ref name="Date" /> [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Col Serra]], a pair of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] turned Rebel officers, were told by [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] about a [[Datacron/Legends|data holocron]] that had been created by [[Viceroy/Legends|Viceroy]] [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. The [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] had been created before the first [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] had [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroyed]] Alderaan, and contained information regarding possible locations for a new base for the Alliance. After the Alliance was forced to retreat from [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] and the Rebel base that had been centered around it, the Rebellion was in dire need of a new central base. As such, the recovery of the holocron was given significant priority by the Alliance. The holocron was known to have been encased in a rare metallic compound known as [[Phrik/Legends|phrik]], one of the strongest substances in the galaxy, and because of this, Ackbar had reason to believe that the holocron might have survived the destruction of Alderaan.<ref name="Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron" />

In response to this information, Ackbar ordered Solo to take a small fleet, along with [[Renegade Squadron/Legends|Renegade Squadron]], the Alliance's recently established fighting unit made up of smugglers and criminals, to [[the Graveyard]], the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] remnants of Alderaan. Under Solo's command were a [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] and two [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigates]], several [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] squadrons, and his own personal ship, the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. Unbeknownst to the Rebels, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] had also discovered the existence and whereabouts of the holocron, and had sent a fleet to the [[Alderaan system/Legends|Alderaan system]] as well. As a contingency plan, the Empire also hired the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to find the holocron in case their forces failed.<ref name="Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron" />

==The battle==
{{Quote|The holocron. You found it. I want it.|Boba Fett talking to the Renegades|<ref name="Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron" />}}
When the Alliance fleet arrived out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], they discovered that there was an Imperial fleet made up of an [[Interdictor-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Interdictor Star Destroyer]] and two [[Victory II-class frigate|''Victory II''-class frigates]] already in the system. The Interdictor launched half of a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]], and in response, Col Serra quickly ordered a member of Renegade Squadron to take out the six incoming TIE fighters using an [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]]. After a small [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfight]], the six TIE fighters were destroyed. However, both sides continued to launch starfighters, escalating the battle.<ref name="Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron" />

As the battle intensified, Solo and his [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] co-[[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], launched the ''Millennium Falcon'' from the Mon Calamari [[Star Cruiser/Legends|Star Cruiser]] to locate and retrieve the holocron. Solo ordered his Renegades to keep fighting the Imperials, hoping to use the battle as a distraction. However, a trio of [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]] followed the ''Falcon'' and managed to damage it. The Renegades were eventually able to destroy the [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptors]], and Solo informed his men that he had found the location of the holocron, but that the ''Falcon'' was too badly damaged to pick it up. As a result, Solo instead ordered Renegade Squadron to retrieve the holocron from the Graveyard and they did so.<ref name="Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron" />

After the Renegades picked up the holocron, Boba Fett arrived into the battle from hyperspace and gave chase in his personal [[Starship/Legends|starship]], [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']]. Realizing that the Empire was about to lose its prize to the Rebels, Fett radioed the Renegades to inform them that he was after the holocron. Understanding that Fett posed a larger threat than the Imperials, Col Serra ordered Renegade Squadron not to allow the bounty hunter to retrieve the holocron. As such, the Renegades engaged Fett, eventually causing enough damage to ''Slave I'' that it forced Fett to leave the battle and jump to hyperspace. The Imperials were now forced to retrieve the holocron themselves.<ref name="Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron" />

With Fett's retreat, the Renegades just had to destroy the Imperial Interdictor's [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]] before they could jump back into hyperspace, so Serra ordered the Rebel forces to attack the [[Battleship/Legends|battleship]]. The Renegades were able to eventually able to destroy the gravity well projectors, and with the way clear, Han Solo led his fleet away from the Graveyard and out of the system.<ref name="Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron" />

==Aftermath==
After the damage sustained in the Alderaan system, Han Solo and Renegade Squadron were ordered to [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]] for repairs. While on the planet, the Renegades were [[Skirmish on Ord Mantell (0.5 ABY)|attacked]] by the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] bounty hunter [[IG-88B|IG-88]] and Imperial forces. After a brief battle, the Renegades defeated the Imperial forces and retreated from the system.<ref name="Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron" />

With the holocron in Rebel hands, Ackbar decided to to personally examine one of the prospective [[Planet/Legends|planets]] on the holocron's list. Ackbar and a small scouting party traveled to the graveyard world of [[Boz Pity/Legends|Boz Pity]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], and after scouting the planet, he decided it would make an ideal spot for a new base. Nonetheless, the Mon Calamari sought to examine other options and took a smaller scouting party with him to [[Saleucami/Legends|Saleucami]], another planet on the list. However, when they exited hyperspace over Saleucami, they were [[Skirmish over Saleucami|ambushed]] by Boba Fett. Fett was able to defeat the Rebels and captured Ackbar and a number of other survivors. Fett then sold Ackbar to the Imperials and presented the other survivors to the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]] on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron" />

==Behind the scenes==
The battle of the Graveyard made its first and only appearance in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron]]'', which recounts the various battles and exploits of Renegade Squadron during the Galactic Civil War. The battle itself makes up the second level in the game's campaign mode.

Due to the fact that the battle takes place only a short time after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the A-wing starfighters that served in this battle could possibly be the [[R-22 Spearhead starfighter|R-22 Spearhead]], the predecessor of the A-wing. However, an official explanation for this has not been presented yet.<ref name="Leland Chee on SW Message Boards">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=93010&start=8519|thread=Books, Comics, & Television VIPs|user=[[Leland Chee]]|date=2004-10-13|time=10:46 AM|check=1}}</ref>
